rule: For the purposes of this market, the term "Ban" means any enforceable prohibition that (a) is enacted by federal legislation, (b) is an order issued by a federal agency with statutory authority, or (c) is a binding court injunction. Non‑binding policy statements, recommendations, or executive orders lacking delegated statutory authority do not constitute a Ban.

"Majority of Americans" shall be interpreted as more than 50 % of the adult United States population who attempted to download TikTok and were prevented from doing so, as evidenced by either (i) official government data, or (ii) a consensus of at least two independent credible news organisations reporting the same blocking statistic.

"Credible reporting" refers to coverage from news outlets that satisfy (i) a long‑standing reputation for factual reporting, (ii) editorial independence, and (iii) corroboration of the reported facts by at least one other independent outlet.

When the US federal government provides an explicit statement regarding a ban, that statement shall prevail as the primary resolution source. If the federal government provides no explicit statement, a Ban documented by a consensus of credible reporting (as defined in Meta‑Rule 3) shall be treated as the primary source.

If TikTok is sold to a United States entity and the sale is approved by CFIUS and the new owner meets all applicable US regulations, this market shall resolve to 'No' regardless of any other conditions.

This market will resolve to 'Yes' if, by 11:59 PM ET on April 30 2025, a Ban (as defined in Meta‑Rule 1) is in effect that prevents download and/or use of the TikTok app for the majority of Americans (as defined in Meta‑Rule 2). Otherwise, this market will resolve to 'No'.

For the condition in Rule 1 to be satisfied, the Ban must be mandated by a federal law, an enforceable federal agency order, or a binding court injunction (i.e., it must meet the definition of a Ban in Meta‑Rule 1).
If TikTok is sold to a United States entity in a manner that results in compliance with all US laws and regulations—including approval by CFIUS—this market shall resolve to 'No'.

The primary source for determining whether the conditions of Rules 1–3 are met shall be official information from the US federal government. In the absence of an explicit federal statement, a consensus of credible reporting (Meta‑Rule 3) shall be used as the primary source.
